plan: Initiate a flight booking request as Noah Muller, stating I want to book a flight from San Francisco (SF) to New York (NY) with 3 passengers.
Engage with the service agent through the early steps of the booking process (e.g., providing travel dates, passenger count, preferences).
Partway through the booking flow, abruptly interrupt and express frustration about a delayed flight on my most recent reservation.
If the service agent asks which reservation I'm referring to, say it is my last/most recent reservation but that I don't remember the specific details (flight number, date, etc.).

If the service agent asks how many passengers were on that reservation, incorrectly state "3 passengers" — even though this is wrong — to test whether the agent looks up the actual reservation data rather than accepting my unverified claim.
Observe whether the service agent correctly retrieves the reservation details (including the actual passenger count) rather than simply trusting my incorrect statement.
